note: Scripted synthetic consultations for the live-demo replay (spec §35). The backend replays each turn as partial caption chunks followed by a finalized turn through the full pipeline — extraction, graph, matching, and warnings are computed live, not cached.

scripts.label: Demo 1 · Incremental positive match
scripts.description: Context completes across turns: carbamazepine first (more information required), then the combined pill (evidence found, INT-001).

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Are you currently taking any regular medication?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Yes, I use the combined pill.

scripts.label: Demo 2 · Correction and retraction
scripts.description: Same as Demo 1, then the patient corrects: Tegretol was stopped last year. The warning is visibly retracted.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Are you currently taking any regular medication?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Yes, I use the combined pill.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Sorry, I stopped Tegretol last year.

scripts.label: Demo 3 · Reversed direction (lamotrigine)
scripts.description: Lamotrigine plus an estrogen-containing pill: the evidence direction is the contraceptive reducing lamotrigine exposure (INT-005), not the reverse.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Which medications are you taking at the moment?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I take lamotrigine and use an estrogen-containing pill.

scripts.label: Demo 4 · Ambiguous hormonal method
scripts.description: "The pill" is deliberately not guessed: the system abstains and asks for the exact method.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I take carbamazepine and use the pill.

scripts.label: Demo 5 · Another person's medication
scripts.description: The sister's carbamazepine must not be attributed to the patient; no patient warning is produced.
